title: Immune System
definition: The body's defense system against pathogens and foreign substances.

difficulty_levels.beginner: A network of cells, tissues, and organs that protect the body from harmful invaders like viruses and bacteria.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A complex system consisting of innate and adaptive immunity, working in concert to detect, eliminate, and remember pathogens and foreign substances.
difficulty_levels.expert: An intricate interplay of immune cells, tissues, and signaling molecules that provides specific and long-lasting defense against a wide variety of threats, while maintaining self/non-self recognition to minimize damage to the host organism.

extra_fields.insight: Nature's intricate system for safeguarding life from harm.
